lessons.title: Historical Development and the Glencar Test
lessons.description: Before 1932, the courts recognised duties of care only in narrow, established categories — between parties in a contractual relationship, or in a small number of defined tortious relationships.

lessons.title: Special Duty Situations — Omissions Third Parties and Public Authorities
lessons.description: Let us begin with omissions. The general rule in negligence is that there is no duty to act — only a duty to refrain from harmful action.

lessons.title: Pure Economic Loss and Negligent Misstatement
lessons.description: The general rule: in negligence, the courts will compensate for physical injury and physical damage to property.
